name: Memory Leak / OOM
diagnosis: System memory exhaustion causing process termination by the OOM-killer
cause: Application level memory leak, unoptimized garbage collection, or underprovisioned RAM
fix_steps: Inspect system syslog or dmesg to identify the terminated PID
Restart the failed service daemon or container
Analyze heap dump files to locate application memory leaks
Increase system swap space or allocate more RAM to the VM

name: CPU Overload
diagnosis: High CPU utilization causing application latency and service degradation
cause: Infinite loop in code, sudden spike in incoming traffic, or resource starvation
fix_steps: Run top/htop to identify the process consuming the most CPU cycles
Profile the active thread stack of the offending process
Scale up CPU cores or scale out nodes behind the load balancer
Set CPU limits and resource quotas on the service container

name: Database Connection Timeout
diagnosis: Application database connection pool depletion or database server unavailability
cause: Network disconnection, database server crash, or database connection pool leak
fix_steps: Ping database host to verify network layer connectivity
Check database server active processes and status of service logs
Scale up database maximum connection settings or pool limit
Review and fix unclosed connections in application code database repository

name: Disk Space Full
diagnosis: Storage capacity exhaustion preventing file write operations
cause: Log rotation failure, runaway application debug logs, or excessive temporary files
fix_steps: Run df -h and du -sh to find the directory consuming the most storage
Purge expired log files or rotate large active logs
Increase the size of the underlying block storage volume
Clean up orphaned docker volumes, build caches, and temp directories

name: Network Disconnect / Packet Loss
diagnosis: High network packet drop rates or complete interface disconnection
cause: Faulty network cabling, hardware failure, routing loop, or firewall blockage
fix_steps: Run traceroute/mtr to locate the hop where packet loss occurs
Verify physical link status and port configuration on the switch
Check routing tables and local firewall rules for blocking patterns
Contact ISP or network provider to report WAN link instability

name: DDoS Attack Detected
diagnosis: Distributed Denial of Service attack overwhelming server ingress bandwidth
cause: Malicious high-volume traffic targeted at public-facing endpoints
fix_steps: Enable rate-limiting and access restriction filters at the web application firewall (WAF)
Blackhole or drop traffic from offending source IP subnets
Redirect traffic through a CDN or cloud-based DDoS mitigation scrub provider
Verify and scale backend capacity to absorb excess load if legitimate

name: SSL Certificate Expired
diagnosis: Invalid SSL/TLS security certificate preventing secure connection establishment
cause: Failure of auto-renewal mechanism, incorrect DNS configuration, or expiration of CA root
fix_steps: Check domain registrar DNS settings and verification records
Manually trigger certificate renewal using certbot or ACME client
Reload the web server configuration to apply newly generated certificate
Configure automated cron jobs for future certificate renewal checks

name: Unauthorized SSH Access Attempt
diagnosis: Brute-force SSH authentication attempts detected on secure port
cause: Server exposed to public internet with default SSH configuration
fix_steps: Disable root SSH login in sshd_config and change port from 22
Install and configure fail2ban to dynamically block repeating IPs
Audit authorized_keys file for unknown public key entries
Enforce SSH key authentication only and restrict allowed IP ranges via firewall

name: DNS Resolution Failure
diagnosis: Inability to resolve domain names to IP addresses
cause: Misconfigured resolv.conf, upstream DNS server outage, or incorrect DNS registration
fix_steps: Test queries using nslookup or dig against reliable resolvers like 8.8.8.8
Check internal resolver service configuration and reload daemon
Verify nameservers listed in /etc/resolv.conf are reachable
Verify domain registry DNS records are published and propagated correctly

name: Normal System Operations
diagnosis: Infrastructure parameters and services are operating normally within parameters
cause: System operating at optimal capacity with no detected failures
fix_steps: Continue monitoring status dashboards for metric trends
Verify automated backups ran successfully in the background
Perform routine non-intrusive software package updates if scheduled
